atoms.semanticSummary:
Base class that makes a data structure iterable and provides common
element-wise utilities (e.g., map/filter/reduce/find).
atoms.technicalSummary:
Base class that makes a data structure iterable and provides common
element-wise utilities (e.g., map/filter/reduce/find).

IterableElementBase. Base class that makes a data structure iterable and provides common
element-wise utilities (e.g., map/filter/reduce/find).

atoms.semanticSummary:
RedBlackTree.setWithHintNode() Insert/update using a hint node to speed up nearby insertions.

close to the expected insertion position (often the previously returned node in a loop).

When the hint is a good fit (sorted / nearly-sorted insertion), this can avoid most of the
normal root-to-leaf search and reduce constant factors.

When the hint does not match (random workloads), this will fall back to the normal set path. Runs in O(log n) time.

TreeMap.createDefaultComparator() Create the strict default comparator.

Supports:
- `number` (rejects `NaN`; treats `-0` and `0` as equal)
- `string`
- `Date` (orders by `getTime()`, rejects invalid dates)

For other key types, a custom comparator must be provided.

atoms.semanticSummary:
TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/...`) return entry tuples like TreeMap.

atoms.semanticSummary:
An ordered Set backed by a red-black tree.

- Iteration order is ascending by key.
- No node exposure: all APIs use keys only.

An ordered Set backed by a red-black tree.

- Iteration order is ascending by key.
- No node exposure: all APIs use keys only.

TreeSet. An ordered Set backed by a red-black tree.

- No node exposure: all APIs use keys only. Tags: tree, hierarchical, recursive, sorted, logarithmic-access

atoms.semanticSummary:
TreeSet.createDefaultComparator() Create the strict default comparator.

Supports:
- `number` (rejects `NaN`; treats `-0` and `0` as equal)
- `string`
- `Date` (orders by `getTime()`, rejects invalid dates)

For other key types, a custom comparator must be provided.

classes.name: TreeMultiMap
classes.semanticSummary:
TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/...`) return entry tuples like TreeMap.

retrievalChunks.title: RedBlackTree Insertion Operations
retrievalChunks.content:
setWithHintNode: Insert/update using a hint node to speed up nearby insertions.

close to the expected insertion position (often the previously returned node in a loop).

When the hint is a good fit (sorted / nearly-sorted insertion), this can avoid most of the
normal root-to-leaf search and reduce constant factors.

When the hint does not match (random workloads), this will fall back to the normal set path. (O(log n)). setWithHint: Boolean wrapper for setWithHintNode. (O(log n)). set: Insert or update a key/value (map mode) or key-only (set mode).

This method is optimized for:
- monotonic inserts via min/max boundary fast paths
- updates via a single-pass search (no double walk) (O(log n))

retrievalChunks.title: TreeMultiMap Overview
retrievalChunks.content:
TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/...`) return entry tuples like TreeMap.

createDefaultComparator: Create the strict default comparator.

Supports:
- `number` (rejects `NaN`; treats `-0` and `0` as equal)
- `string`
- `Date` (orders by `getTime()`, rejects invalid dates)

For other key types, a custom comparator must be provided.. has: Test whether a key exists. (O(log n)). [Symbol.iterator]: . find: Find the first value that satisfies a predicate. (O(n)). rangeSearch: Return all keys in a given range.

retrievalChunks.embeddingText: Trie deletion operations: clear: Remove all words and reset to a fresh root. (O(1)). delete: Delete one word if present. (O(L))

retrievalChunks.title: Trie Lookup Operations
retrievalChunks.content: has: Check whether a word exists. (O(L)). getHeight: Compute the height (max depth) of the trie. (O(N)). hasPurePrefix: Check whether input is a proper prefix of at least one word. (O(L)). hasPrefix: Check whether any word starts with input. (O(L)). hasCommonPrefix: Check whether the trie’s longest common prefix equals input. (O(min(H,L)). getLongestCommonPrefix: Return the longest common prefix among all words. (O(H)). getWords: Collect words under a prefix up to a maximum count. (O(K·L))
retrievalChunks.embeddingText: Trie lookup operations: has: Check whether a word exists. (O(L)). getHeight: Compute the height (max depth) of the trie. (O(N)). hasPurePrefix: Check whether input is a proper prefix of at least one word. (O(L)). hasPrefix: Check whether any word starts with input. (O(L)). hasCommonPrefix: Check whether the trie’s longest common prefix equals input. (O(min(H,L)). getLongestCommonPrefix: Return the longest common prefix among all words. (O(H)). getWords: Collect words under a prefix up to a maximum count. (O(K·L))

qa.instruction: How do I set with hint node in RedBlackTree?
qa.output:
Use RedBlackTree.setWithHintNode(). RedBlackTree.setWithHintNode() Insert/update using a hint node to speed up nearby insertions.

close to the expected insertion position (often the previously returned node in a loop).

When the hint is a good fit (sorted / nearly-sorted insertion), this can avoid most of the
normal root-to-leaf search and reduce constant factors.

When the hint does not match (random workloads), this will fall back to the normal set path. Runs in O(log n) time.

qa.instruction: How do I set in RedBlackTree?
qa.output:
Use RedBlackTree.set(). RedBlackTree.set() Insert or update a key/value (map mode) or key-only (set mode).

This method is optimized for:
- monotonic inserts via min/max boundary fast paths
- updates via a single-pass search (no double walk) Runs in O(log n) time.

qa.instruction: What is TreeMultiMap in data-structure-typed?
qa.output:
TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/...`) return entry tuples like TreeMap.

The queue removes the smallest element first under the provided comparator.
Provide a custom comparator if you store non-primitive objects.

qa.instruction: What is Deque in data-structure-typed?

qa.instruction: When should I use Deque?

qa.instruction: What is Queue in data-structure-typed?

qa.instruction: When should I use Queue?

qa.instruction: What is Trie in data-structure-typed?

qa.instruction: What is the difference between Stack and Queue?
qa.output: Stack is LIFO (last-in-first-out) while Queue is FIFO (first-in-first-out).

qa.instruction: What is the difference between Deque and Queue?
qa.output: Deque allows insertion and deletion at both ends, while Queue only at front/back.

qa.instruction: What is the difference between TreeMap and HashMap?
qa.output: TreeMap maintains sorted key order with O(log n) ops, HashMap is unordered with O(1) average.

qa.instruction: What is the difference between DoublyLinkedList and SinglyLinkedList?
qa.output: DoublyLinkedList has prev pointers allowing bidirectional traversal, SinglyLinkedList only has next pointers.

qa.instruction: What is the difference between RedBlackTree and AVLTree?
qa.output: Both are self-balancing. AVL is more strictly balanced (faster lookups), Red-Black has faster insertions.
